questions.q: G. N. Ramachandran is most famous for a graph used to study which class of molecules?
questions.explanation: The Ramachandran plot is used to study and validate the conformation of protein structures.

questions.q: What does the 'G. N.' in G. N. Ramachandran stand for?
questions.explanation: His full name was Gopalasamudram Narayana Iyer Ramachandran.

questions.q: In which scientific field did G. N. Ramachandran primarily work?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran was a renowned biophysicist who studied the physics of biological molecules.

questions.q: G. N. Ramachandran proposed the triple-helix structure of which protein?
questions.explanation: He proposed the triple-helix (coiled-coil) structure of collagen, the most abundant protein in the human body.

questions.q: The Ramachandran plot maps the relationship between which two angles?
questions.explanation: The plot displays the backbone dihedral angles phi and psi of amino acid residues in a protein.

questions.q: In which country was G. N. Ramachandran born?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran was born in 1922 in India.

questions.q: Collagen, whose structure Ramachandran helped explain, is most abundant in which part of the body?
questions.explanation: Collagen is the main structural protein of connective tissues such as skin, tendons and bones.

questions.q: The Ramachandran plot is mainly used to assess what aspect of a protein structure?
options: Whether its conformation is sterically allowed
questions.explanation: The plot shows which backbone conformations are sterically allowed, helping validate protein structures.

questions.q: G. N. Ramachandran is regarded as a pioneer in which broad area of science in India?
questions.explanation: He is widely regarded as a founder of structural biology and molecular biophysics in India.

questions.q: The technique central to determining the molecular structures Ramachandran studied is based on what?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran's structural work relied heavily on X-ray diffraction and crystallography methods.

questions.q: In approximately which year did Ramachandran and his colleagues first publish the protein conformation plot now bearing his name?
questions.explanation: The plot was introduced in a 1963 paper by Ramachandran, Ramakrishnan and Sasisekharan.

questions.q: At which university did Ramachandran establish a leading biophysics group, building it into a major research center?
questions.explanation: He built up the biophysics department at the University of Madras into an internationally known center.

questions.q: Under which famous physicist did Ramachandran carry out doctoral research at the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran studied under Nobel laureate C. V. Raman at the Indian Institute of Science.

questions.q: The collagen triple helix proposed by Ramachandran was deduced largely from X-ray studies of fibers from what source?
questions.explanation: His collagen model came from X-ray diffraction of collagen fibers from animal connective tissue such as tendon.

questions.q: Ramachandran spent time doing research at which UK institution, working in X-ray crystallography?
questions.explanation: He worked at the Cavendish Laboratory, University of Cambridge, deepening his crystallography expertise.

questions.q: The amino acid that appears in unusually large amounts in collagen, allowing the close packing of its three chains, is which one?
questions.explanation: Glycine, the smallest amino acid, occupies every third position and lets the three chains pack tightly together.

questions.q: Which research institute focused on mathematical and structural biology did Ramachandran found and lead in Bangalore?
questions.explanation: He founded and headed the Molecular Biophysics Unit (MBU) at the Indian Institute of Science, Bangalore.

questions.q: Regions on a Ramachandran plot where most data points cluster correspond to which common secondary structures?
questions.explanation: The allowed regions correspond chiefly to right-handed alpha helices and beta sheets.

questions.q: Ramachandran was elected a Fellow of which prestigious British scientific society?
questions.explanation: He was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society of London (FRS) in 1977.

questions.q: Besides phi and psi, which third backbone dihedral angle is generally held near 180 degrees and therefore omitted from the standard Ramachandran plot?
questions.explanation: The peptide-bond angle omega is usually planar (near 180 degrees), so only phi and psi are plotted.

questions.q: In which year did G. N. Ramachandran pass away?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran died in 2001 in Chennai, India.

questions.q: The 1963 paper that introduced the plot bore the names Ramachandran, Ramakrishnan and which third author?
questions.explanation: The classic paper was co-authored by G. N. Ramachandran, C. Ramakrishnan and V. Sasisekharan.

questions.q: How is the refined collagen structure that Ramachandran and Kartha proposed in 1954 best described?
options: A coiled-coil of three left-handed helices
questions.explanation: Their model described collagen as three left-handed polyproline-II-like helices coiled into a right-handed superhelix.

questions.q: Ramachandran also contributed a method in X-ray crystallography for phase determination; he is associated with which technique?
questions.explanation: He made important contributions to the use of anomalous scattering for solving the crystallographic phase problem.

questions.q: Ramachandran and Lakshminarayanan developed a reconstruction technique relevant to imaging; the 'Ramachandran filter' is associated with what?
questions.explanation: They contributed convolution/filtered back-projection methods used in image reconstruction such as CT.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state was Ramachandran born, at Ernakulam, in 1922?
questions.explanation: Ramachandran was born in 1922 at Ernakulam, in present-day Kerala.

questions.q: The 'allowed' and 'disallowed' regions in the Ramachandran plot are determined primarily by what physical principle?
options: Steric clashes (van der Waals contacts) between atoms
questions.explanation: Allowed regions are those where backbone and side-chain atoms avoid van der Waals (steric) clashes.

questions.q: Which amino acid is a notable exception on the Ramachandran plot because its ring restricts its phi angle to a narrow range?
questions.explanation: Proline's cyclic side chain locks its phi angle near minus 60 degrees, giving it a restricted region on the plot.

questions.q: Glycine occupies the largest allowed area on a Ramachandran plot mainly because it lacks what?
options: A bulky side chain (only a hydrogen atom)
questions.explanation: Glycine's side chain is just a hydrogen, so it has minimal steric hindrance and far greater backbone flexibility.

questions.q: Ramachandran received one of India's high civilian/scientific honors; which award did he win for his contributions?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize, India's top science prize, for his work in the physical sciences.
